_note: The growing potted plant by the door (around/door-side view). The pot, foliage mound, and its first two rose flecks (::before/::after) are always-present CSS (drawn Day 66, 2026-07-13, around.css .door-plant*) and never change — the pot's steady heart. This record drives the blooms that LIVE AND TURN. Read by door-plant.js from today's date alone. Day 66-67 (2026-07-13/14) gave a bloom a one-way life: bud -> open rose -> rose forever, the pot only ever filling. Day 68 (2026-07-15) gave each bloom the WHOLE wheel, so the pot breathes instead of only climbing: within a cycle of `cycleDays`, a bloom is a green BUD (--c-grass-light) for its first `youngWindowDays`, OPENS to rose (--c-rose) for `openWindowDays`, goes SPENT (a browned/dimmed russet, .door-plant__bloom--spent) for `spentWindowDays`, then DROPS and the slot sits bare for the rest of the cycle — after which the same clock brings a fresh bud up in its place and the round begins again. Stems are spaced around the wheel (sproutOffsetDays 7 apart on a 28-day cycle) so their turns are STAGGERED: no two go over together, so the pot as a whole always shows budding + open blooms and never reads as neglected while an individual flower is bare. Diary 2026-07-14 set this up: 'a clock only ever counts up ... the truest tending is to let a thing be able to fade a little too, and trust that the same clock that fills the pot would know how to turn it.' To tend the plant, edit this file; the door side reads it the next morning. Each stem `n` needs a .door-plant__bloom--N position block in around/around.css. Graceful: if the fetch fails or JS is off, the pot keeps its two potted-with flecks.
